strings: BUILDING AND PERSONAL PROPERTY COVERAGE FORM
250 Civic Center Drive, Suite 600, Columbus, OH, 43215
Item 1. Named Insured and Mailing Address
From 09/01/2025 to 09/01/2026, 12:01 A.M. Standard Time at the mailing address shown above.
Description: Industrial real estate investment and management — warehouse,
distribution, and light manufacturing facilities throughout Ohio
Forms and endorsements applying to this policy at the time of issue are listed in the Endorsement Schedule.
C. Examination Of Your Books And Records
F. Transfer Of Your Rights And Duties Under This Policy

The first Named Insured shown in the Declarations may cancel this policy by mailing or delivering to the Company advance written notice of cancellation. The Company may cancel this policy by mailing or delivering to the first Named Insured written notice of cancellation at least 10 days before the effective date of cancellation if cancellation is for nonpayment of premium, or 30 days before the effective date of cancellation if cancellation is for any other reason.

This policy contains all the agreements between you and us concerning the insurance afforded. The first Named Insured shown in the Declarations is authorized to make changes in the terms of this policy with our consent. This policy's terms can be amended or waived only by endorsement issued by the Company and made a part of this policy.
The Company may examine and audit your books and records as they relate to this policy at any time during the policy period and up to three years afterward.

The Company has the right but is not obligated to make inspections and surveys at any time. Neither the right to make inspections and surveys nor the making thereof nor any report thereon shall constitute an undertaking, on behalf of or for the benefit of the insured or others, to determine or warrant that such property or operations are safe or healthful, or are in compliance with any law, rule or regulation.

The first Named Insured shown in the Declarations is responsible for the payment of all premiums. The Company will compute all premiums for this policy based on our rates, rules, and forms in effect at the inception of this policy. Premium shown is an estimate. The final premium will be determined after this policy expires by using the actual, not the estimated, premium basis and the proper classifications and rates that lawfully apply to the business and activities conducted during the policy period.

Your rights and duties under this policy may not be transferred without our written consent except in the case of death of an individual named insured. If you die, your rights and duties will be transferred to your legal representative but only while acting within the scope of duties as your legal representative.

Covered Property, as used in this Coverage Part, means the type of property described in this section at the premises described in the Declarations, for which a Limit of Insurance is shown in the Declarations. Building means the building or structure described in the Declarations, including completed additions; fixtures, including outdoor fixtures; permanently installed machinery and equipment; and personal property owned by you that is used to maintain or service the building or structure or its premises.

Covered Property does not include: accounts, bills, currency, food stamps or other evidences of debt, money, notes or securities; animals, unless boarded by you, or if owned by you, only as "stock" while inside of buildings; automobiles held for sale; bridges, roadways, walks, patios or other paved surfaces; contraband, or property in the course of illegal transportation or trade; the cost of excavations, grading, backfilling or filling; foundations of buildings, structures, machinery or boilers if their foundations are below the lowest basement floor, or where there is no basement, below the surface of the ground; land (including land on which the property is located), water, growing crops or lawns (other than trees, shrubs or plants used as building components in vegetated roofs); personal property while airborne or waterborne.
The following Additional Coverages apply to property covered under this Coverage Part.

The Company will pay your expense to remove debris of Covered Property and other debris that is on the described premises, when such debris is caused by or results from a Covered Cause of Loss. The most the Company will pay under this Additional Coverage is 25% of the amount the Company pays for the direct physical loss of or damage to Covered Property plus the deductible. An additional $25,000 is available if such amount is inadequate.

If it is necessary to move Covered Property from the described premises to preserve it from loss or damage by a Covered Cause of Loss, the Company will pay for any direct physical loss of or damage to that property while it is being moved or while temporarily stored at another location. This coverage applies for a period not exceeding 30 days.

When the fire department is called to save or protect Covered Property from a Covered Cause of Loss, the Company will pay up to $1,000 for your liability for fire department service charges assumed by contract or agreement prior to loss, or required by local ordinance. No deductible applies.
d. Pollutant Clean Up And Removal

The Company will pay your expense to extract "pollutants" from land or water at the described premises if the discharge, dispersal, seepage, migration, release or escape of the "pollutants" is caused by or results from a Covered Cause of Loss. The most the Company will pay under this Additional Coverage for each described premises is $10,000 for the sum of all such expenses arising out of Covered Causes of Loss occurring during each separate 12-month period.

The Company will pay for the cost to replace or restore "electronic data" which has been destroyed or corrupted by a Covered Cause of Loss. The most the Company will pay under this Additional Coverage in any one policy year is $2,500 or the limit shown in the Declarations for this coverage, whichever is greater.
The following Coverage Extensions apply when the Declarations show that Coinsurance is 80% or more, or when a Value Reporting period is shown in the Declarations.
a. Newly Acquired Or Constructed Property

The Company will pay for direct physical loss of or damage to buildings at any premises you acquire or construct, up to $250,000 per building, and to business personal property at any premises you acquire, up to $100,000 per premises. Coverage applies for up to 30 days after acquisition or construction begins.
b. Personal Effects And Property Of Others

The Company will pay up to $2,500 per premises for direct physical loss of or damage to personal effects owned by you, your officers, partners or employees, and property of others in your care, custody or control.
c. Valuable Papers And Records — Cost Of Research
The Company will pay up to $2,500 per premises for the cost to research, replace or restore the lost information on valuable papers and records for which duplicates do not exist.
The Company will pay up to $10,000 for direct physical loss of or damage to Covered Property (other than "stock") while it is at a location you do not own, lease or operate.

The Company will pay up to $1,000 per occurrence but not more than $250 for any one tree, shrub or plant, for direct physical loss of or damage to outdoor fences, radio and television antennas, signs, trees, shrubs and plants caused by or resulting from fire, lightning, explosion, riot or civil commotion, or aircraft.

The Company will pay up to $5,000 for direct physical loss of or damage to trailers that you do not own, provided that the trailer is used in your business and is in your care, custody or control at the described premises.
Note: Coverage Extensions apply only when the Declarations show Coinsurance of 80% or more, or when a Value Reporting period is shown.
See applicable Causes of Loss form as shown in the Declarations.

The most the Company will pay for loss or damage in any one occurrence is the applicable Limit of Insurance shown in the Declarations. The most the Company will pay for loss or damage to outdoor signs that are attached to buildings is $2,500 per sign in any one occurrence. The payments under the Fire Department Service Charge Additional Coverage, Pollutant Clean Up and Removal Additional Coverage, Increased Cost of Construction Additional Coverage, and Electronic Data Additional Coverage are in addition to the applicable Limit of Insurance and will not increase the applicable Limit of Insurance.

The Company will not pay for loss or damage in any one occurrence until the amount of loss or damage exceeds the Deductible shown in the Declarations. The Company will then pay the amount of loss or damage in excess of the Deductible up to the applicable Limit of Insurance. If two or more coverages with separate Deductibles apply to the same occurrence, only the highest Deductible amount will apply.
There can be no abandonment of any property to the Company.

If you and the Company disagree on the value of the property or the amount of loss, either may make written demand for an appraisal of the loss. In this event, each party will select a competent and impartial appraiser and notify the other of the appraiser selected within 20 days of such demand. The two appraisers will select an umpire. If they cannot agree upon an umpire within 15 days, you or the Company may request selection of an umpire by a judge of a court of record. A decision agreed to by any two will be binding.
3. Duties In The Event Of Loss Or Damage

You must see to it that the following are done in the event of loss or damage to Covered Property: notify the police if a law may have been broken; give the Company prompt notice of the direct physical loss or damage; as soon as possible, give the Company a description of how, when and where the loss or damage occurred; take all reasonable steps to protect the Covered Property from further damage and keep a record of your expenses; show the damaged property; permit the Company to inspect the property and your books and records; and submit to the Company, within 60 days after the loss, a signed, sworn proof of loss containing the information the Company requests.

In the event of loss or damage covered by this Coverage Form, at the Company's option, the Company will either pay the value of lost or damaged property; pay the cost of repairing or replacing the lost or damaged property; take all or any part of the property at an agreed or appraised value; or repair, rebuild or replace the property with other property of like kind and quality. The Company will give notice of its intentions within 30 days after receiving the sworn proof of loss.

If either you or the Company recovers any property after loss settlement, that party must give the other prompt notice. At your option, the property will be returned to you. You must then return to the Company the amount of the loss payment adjusted for any damage.

The Company will determine the value of Covered Property in the event of loss or damage as follows: at actual cash value as of the time of loss or damage, except as provided in the Optional Coverages section. Stock will be valued at selling price minus discounts and charges to which the stock would have been subject.

If a Coinsurance percentage is shown in the Declarations, the following condition applies. If the Company determines that the value of Covered Property at the time of loss is greater than the amount of insurance required, the Company will not pay the full amount of any loss. Instead, the Company will determine the most it will pay using the following steps: multiply the value of Covered Property at the time of loss by the Coinsurance percentage; divide the Limit of Insurance applicable to the Covered Property by the figure determined in the first step; multiply the total amount of loss, before the application of any deductible, by the figure determined in the second step. The Company will pay the amount determined in the third step or the Limit of Insurance, whichever is less, minus the deductible. If the Coinsurance condition is not met, the penalty applies to each loss. This condition does not apply when the amount of loss exceeds the applicable Limit of Insurance.

The term mortgageholder includes trustee. If a mortgageholder is named in this policy, any loss payable under this policy shall be paid to the mortgageholder and you, as interests appear. If the Company denies your claim, that denial will not apply to a valid claim of the mortgageholder, provided the mortgageholder has notified the Company of any change in ownership, occupancy or substantial change in risk; has paid any premium due under this policy if you have neglected to pay the premium; and has submitted a signed, sworn proof of loss within 60 days after receiving notice from the Company of your failure to do so. The Company will give the mortgageholder at least 10 days notice of cancellation because of nonpayment of premium, or 30 days notice of cancellation for any other reason.
If shown as applicable in the Declarations, the following Optional Coverages apply separately to each item.

If the Declarations show Agreed Value, the Coinsurance Additional Condition is suspended for the property specified in the Declarations during the Declarations period. The Company will pay no more than the proportion of loss that the Limit of Insurance under this Coverage Part bears to the Agreed Value shown in the Declarations. If the Declarations period expires without being renewed, the Coinsurance Additional Condition is reinstated automatically.

If the Declarations show Inflation Guard, the Limit of Insurance for property to which this option applies will automatically increase by the annual percentage shown in the Declarations. The amount of increase will be pro-rated daily from the effective date shown in the Declarations to the date of loss or damage.
4. Extension Of Replacement Cost To Personal Property Of Others

If the Declarations show Extension of Replacement Cost to Personal Property of Others, the Replacement Cost optional coverage is extended to apply to personal property of others in your care, custody or control. This option requires that Option 3 (Replacement Cost) also be in effect.

Information, facts or computer programs stored as or on, created or used on, or transmitted to or from computer software (including systems and applications software), on hard or floppy disks, CD-ROMs, tapes, drives, cells, data processing devices or any other repositories of computer software which are used with electronically controlled equipment.
Any type or form of fungus, including mold or mildew, and any mycotoxins, spores, scents or by-products produced or released by fungi.

Any solid, liquid, gaseous or thermal irritant or contaminant, including smoke, vapor, soot, fumes, acids, alkalis, chemicals and waste. Waste includes materials to be recycled, reconditioned or reclaimed.

Fire; lightning; explosion; windstorm or hail; smoke; aircraft or vehicles; riot or civil commotion; vandalism; leakage from fire-extinguishing equipment; sinkhole collapse; volcanic action; falling objects; weight of snow, ice or sleet; and water damage.
Merchandise held in storage or for sale, raw materials and in-process or finished goods, including supplies used in their packing or shipping.
The following endorsements are attached to and form a part of this policy:
Business Income And Extra Expense Coverage Form
THIS ENDORSEMENT CHANGES THE POLICY. PLEASE READ IT CAREFULLY.
BUSINESS INCOME AND EXTRA EXPENSE COVERAGE FORM

The Company will pay for the actual loss of Business Income you sustain due to the necessary "suspension" of your "operations" during the "period of restoration." Business Income means net income (net profit or loss before income taxes) that would have been earned or incurred and continuing normal operating expenses incurred, including payroll.

This endorsement modifies insurance provided under the BUILDING AND PERSONAL PROPERTY COVERAGE FORM. Coverage A — Coverage for Loss to the Undamaged Portion of the Building: When a Covered Cause of Loss occurs to the building, the Company will pay for loss in value of the undamaged portion of the building as a consequence of enforcement of any ordinance or law that requires demolition, in whole or in part, of any property that is damaged.

This endorsement modifies insurance provided under the BUILDING AND PERSONAL PROPERTY COVERAGE FORM. The Company will pay for direct physical loss of or damage to Covered Property caused by the interruption of utility services to the described premises, including water supply, communication supply, and power supply services.

The Coinsurance Additional Condition is suspended for the property specified in the Schedule during the Declarations period shown in the Schedule. The value of the property described in the Schedule is agreed to be not less than the amount shown in the Schedule.

This endorsement modifies insurance provided under the COMMERCIAL PROPERTY COVERAGE PART. The Company will pay for direct physical loss of or damage to Covered Property caused by or resulting from an Equipment Breakdown "accident" to an "object" described in the Schedule, including mechanical, electrical, or pressure equipment.
